text: Writing clean code is an essential skill for any programmer. Clean code is easy to read and understand, which makes it simpler to maintain and update in the future. One key rule is to use clear naming conventions: variable names like `userAge` are much better than `x` or `temp`. Also, it is important to break your program into small, focused functions. Each function should do only one task. This makes your code more organized and testable. When you follow these practices, you not only help yourself, but you also help other developers who might work with your code. In short, clean code saves time and reduces frustration.
